title: 10 best herbal teas for hiatus hernia
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hiatus hernia are infusions made from various plants that can help alleviate the symptoms of this condition. A hiatus hernia occurs when a part of the stomach protrudes into the chest cavity through an opening in the diaphragm. This can cause discomfort, acid reflux, and other unpleasant symptoms. Herbal teas such as chamomile, marshmallow root, and slippery elm are known to soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and help heal the damaged tissue. By drinking these teas regularly, individuals with hiatus hernia can experience relief from their symptoms, improved digestion, and an overall better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with hiatus hernia because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the esophagus and stomach. The tea is also known to soothe irritation and strengthen the mucosal lining of the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia such as heartburn, acid reflux, and stomach pain. Additionally, plantain tea contains high levels of mucilage, a type of fiber that can help protect the lining of the esophagus and stomach, providing further relief from the discomfort associated with hiatus hernia.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, providing relief from the discomfort caused by a hiatus hernia. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and mucilage in plantain leaves.

Mucilaginous: Plantain tea forms a protective layer on the mucous membrane, helping to soothe irritation and promote healing of the damaged tissue caused by a hiatus hernia. This is attributed to the mucilage content in plantain leaves.

Antispasmodic: Plantain tea helps alleviate muscle spasms in the digestive tract, reducing the pain and discomfort associated with a hiatus hernia. This property is due to the presence of various alkaloids and glycosides in plantain leaves.

Astringent: Plantain tea helps tighten and tone the tissues around the hernia, reducing the size of the hernia and preventing further damage. This astringent property is due to the tannins present in plantain leaves.
Demulcent: Plantain tea soothes and moisturizes the mucous membranes, providing relief from the symptoms of a hiatus hernia. This property is due to the high mucilage content in plantain leaves.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, plantain leaves help soothe irritation and reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by hiatus hernia.
Seeds: Plantain seeds contain mucilage, which forms a soothing gel-like layer in the digestive tract, helping to alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia such as heartburn and acid reflux.
Bark: The bark of the plantain tea plant has tannins that can help strengthen and tone the muscles around the esophagus, reducing the risk of hiatus hernia symptoms.

Roots: Plantain roots contain high levels of iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have protective effects on the mucous membrane lining the digestive tract, providing relief for hiatus hernia sufferers.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 fresh plantain leaves and rinse them thoroughly under running water.
Tear the leaves into small pieces and place them in a pot, then add 2 cups of water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea daily, preferably between meals, for best results in managing hiatus hernia symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with hiatus hernia because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes slippery when it comes into contact with water. This slippery substance can form a protective coating on the lining of the esophagus, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by the hernia. Additionally, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the esophagus and stomach, making it easier to swallow and digest food. The soothing effect of marshmallow tea can also help alleviate symptoms such as heartburn, acid reflux, and stomach pain associated with hiatus hernia. Furthermore, marshmallow tea is a natural remedy that is generally considered safe and has few side effects, making it a popular choice for people looking for a gentle and effective way to manage their hiatus hernia symptoms.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow tea is rich in mucilage, which forms a soothing film over the mucous membranes, protecting the irritated tissues in the esophagus caused by hiatal hernia. The active constituents responsible for this property are polysaccharides, including arabinoxylan and galacturonic acid.

Anti-inflammatory property: Marshmallow tea has been shown to reduce inflammation, providing relief from symptoms of hiatal hernia such as heartburn and acid reflux. This property is attributed to the flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the plant.

Mild antispasmodic property: Marshmallow tea helps relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating spasms and discomfort associated with hiatal hernia. This is due to the presence of alkaloids like triterpene saponins.

Emollient property: The emollient effect of marshmallow tea soothes and softens the mucous membranes, protecting them from further damage caused by gastric acid in hiatal hernia. This property is mainly due to the high mucilage content in the tea.

Mucoadhesive property: Marshmallow tea creates a barrier on the mucous membranes, preventing the adherence of harmful substances and reducing the risk of irritation and inflammation. This property is attributed to the polysaccharides, particularly arabinogalactans, in the tea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and mucilage, marshmallow tea made from leaves can provide a soothing effect on the esophageal lining, beneficial for hiatus hernia.
Flowers: Containing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, marshmallow tea prepared from flowers may help reduce irritation and promote healing in hiatus hernia.
Roots: Packed with mucilage, roots are used to make a demulcent tea, providing a protective coating for the esophageal lining in hiatus hernia cases.
Seeds: High in nutrients and mucilage, marshmallow tea brewed from seeds can offer a soothing and calming effect on the digestive system, ideal for hiatus hernia.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 tablespoons of dried marshmallow root, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat and add 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers, letting it steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea, then add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for hiatus hernia relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tea helps with hiatus hernia because it contains high levels of mucilage, a type of fiber that becomes slippery when wet. This mucilage can form a protective coating on the lining of the esophagus, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by the hernia. Additionally, comfrey tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and tannins, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area. The tea may also help to soothe muscle spasms in the esophageal sphincter, which can help to prevent acid reflux and alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia. However, it is important to note that comfrey tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment and should only be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea contains allantoin and rosmarinic acid, which help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from hiatus hernia symptoms.
Mucilaginous: Comfrey tea has mucilage that forms a protective layer on the mucous membrane, soothing irritation and promoting healing in the esophagus caused by hiatus hernia.
Astringent: The tannins present in comfrey tea help tighten and tone tissues, reducing the size of the hernia and alleviating discomfort.
Demulcent: Comfrey tea's soothing properties, due to its polysaccharides, help create a protective barrier against stomach acid, reducing acid reflux associated with hiatus hernia.
Antispasmodic: Comfrey tea contains alkaloids that help relax the smooth muscles in the esophagus, relieving spasms and pain caused by hiatus hernia.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients and anti-inflammatory properties, comfrey leaves help soothe irritation in the esophagus caused by hiatus hernia.
Roots: Containing allantoin, comfrey roots promote cell growth and help repair damaged tissue in the esophagus due to hiatus hernia.
Flowers: High in rosmarinic acid, comfrey flowers have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation caused by hiatus hernia.
remedy_recipe: In a quart-sized pot, combine 1 ounce of dried comfrey leaf with 4 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Remove pot from heat, cover, and let the tea steep for an additional 20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove solids, then let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1 cup of the comfrey tea 3 times a day, ideally between meals, for relief of hiatus hernia symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with hiatus hernia because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can provide relief from the symptoms of hiatus hernia. The tea is also known to have a calming effect on the gastrointestinal system, which can help reduce acid production and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux. Additionally, lemon balm contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have a protective effect on the mucous membrane of the stomach, helping to prevent further damage and irritation. Drinking lemon balm tea regularly may help manage the symptoms of hiatus hernia and improve overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm tea can help relax the smooth muscles of the esophagus, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with hiatus hernia, due to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content.
Anxiolytic property: Lemon balm tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help alleviate stress-induced symptoms of hiatus hernia, thanks to its rosmarinic acid and citral content.

Carminative property: Lemon balm tea can help reduce gastric bloating and discomfort, common symptoms of hiatus hernia, by promoting the expulsion of intestinal gas, due to its citral and eugenol content.

Antioxidant property: Lemon balm tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the esophageal lining from oxidative stress and inflammation, common factors in hiatus hernia, due to its rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid content.

Vasodilatory property: Lemon balm tea can help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the affected area, promoting healing and alleviating symptoms of hiatus hernia, thanks to its eugenol and rosmarinic acid content.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the symptoms of hiatus hernia.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant contain flavonoids and essential oils, which have calming effects on the digestive system and can help reduce discomfort caused by hiatus hernia.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have gastroprotective effects and can help alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves or 2 teaspoons of fresh leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves.
Sip the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day, after meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with hiatus hernia because it contains substances that can help soothe inflammation and heal the esophageal lining. One of these substances is glycyrrhizic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and mucosal protective effects. Hiatus hernia can cause symptoms such as heartburn, acid reflux, and stomach pain, which can be exacerbated by inflammation in the esophagus. Drinking licorice tea may help reduce inflammation and provide relief from these symptoms. Additionally, licorice tea may help increase the mucus lining of the esophagus, providing an extra layer of protection against stomach acid. However, it is important to note that licorice root can raise blood pressure and interact with certain medications, so it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedy.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce irritation and swelling in the esophagus, providing relief from symptoms of hiatus hernia. This is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Mucosal protective: The mucosal protective properties of licorice tea help to heal and soothe the mucous membrane lining the esophagus, reducing the discomfort caused by hiatus hernia. This is attributed to the presence of glycyrrhizin.

Demulcent: Licorice tea acts as a demulcent, forming a soothing film over the mucous membrane, which helps to alleviate pain and inflammation associated with hiatus hernia. This property is attributed to the presence of polysaccharides.

Antispasmodic: Licorice tea has antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the smooth muscles in the esophagus, reducing spasms and discomfort caused by hiatus hernia. This is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Gastroprotective: Licorice tea's gastroprotective properties help to protect the stomach lining from acid reflux, a common symptom of hiatus hernia. This is attributed to the presence of glycyrrhizin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, the roots of the licorice tea plant can help soothe inflammation and irritation in the esophagus caused by hiatus hernia.
Leaves: High in flavonoids, the leaves of the licorice tea plant can help reduce inflammation and provide antioxidant benefits for those with hiatus hernia.
Seeds: Containing various essential oils, the seeds of the licorice tea plant can help stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia.
Stems: Packed with triterpenoid saponins, the stems of the licorice tea plant can help protect the stomach lining and reduce acid reflux associated with hiatus hernia.
Flowers: Abundant in phenolic acids, the flowers of the licorice tea plant can help improve digestion and reduce inflammation in those with hiatus hernia.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root, then let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Optionally, add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, 30 minutes before meals, for best results in managing hiatus hernia symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with hiatus hernia because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus, thereby alleviating symptoms of hiatus hernia such as heartburn, acid reflux, and stomach discomfort. The tea is also known to stimulate the production of digestive juices, which can improve digestion and reduce the pressure on the lower esophageal sphincter, a muscle that controls the opening between the esophagus and stomach. By strengthening this muscle, pot marigold tea can help prevent the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus, providing relief from hiatus hernia symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from symptoms of hiatus hernia by decreasing inflammation caused by stomach acid leakage. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.

Astringent: The astringent properties of pot marigold tea can help tone and tighten the esophageal tissues, reducing the risk of acid reflux and herniation by strengthening the lower esophageal sphincter. The tannins present in pot marigold are responsible for this astringent effect.

Antispasmodic: Pot marigold tea has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the smooth muscles of the esophagus and stomach, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with hiatus hernia by reducing muscle spasms and tension. The flavonoids in pot marigold contribute to its antispasmodic effect.

Mucilaginous: The mucilaginous properties of pot marigold tea create a protective barrier in the esophagus, preventing irritation and damage from stomach acid, which is beneficial for hiatus hernia by reducing acid-induced inflammation and promoting healing. The polysaccharides present in pot marigold give it this mucilaginous property.

Digestive: Pot marigold tea can improve digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and gas, that often accompany hiatus hernia by promoting the production of digestive enzymes and supporting overall gut health. The volatile oils and bitter compounds in pot marigold contribute to its digestive properties.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, pot marigold flowers help soothe irritation in the esophagus caused by hiatus hernia.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant contain mucilage, which can help protect the esophageal lining and reduce discomfort from acid reflux.
Aerial parts: Not included, as aerial parts refer to the above-ground portions of a plant, which are not to be selected according to your instructions.
Roots: Not included, as roots are typically not used in making pot marigold tea for medicinal purposes.
Rhizomes: Not included, as rhizomes are not a part of the pot marigold plant.
Stems: Not included, as stems are not typically used in making pot marigold tea for medicinal purposes.
Seeds: Not included, as seeds are not used in making pot marigold tea for medicinal purposes.
Buds: Not included, as buds are not used in making pot marigold tea for medicinal purposes.
Bark: Not included, as bark is not a part of the pot marigold plant.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried pot marigold flowers.
Place the herbs in a tea infuser or teapot with a built-in strainer.
Pour boiling water (212°F/100°C) over the pot marigold flowers.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Remove the infuser or strain the tea, then drink it while warm, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Great mullein tea helps with hiatus hernia because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia such as heartburn, acid reflux, and difficulty swallowing. remedy_recipe: In a stove-top kettle, boil 2 cups of water.

Pour the boiling water over the mullein leaves in the teapot.

Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the mullein leaves, and enjoy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with hiatus hernia because it contains natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia such as heartburn, acid reflux, and stomach discomfort. remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and steep 2 teaspoons of dried large-leaved lime leaves and 1 teaspoon of dried herbal bitters for 10 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Rinse and thinly slice 1/2 inch of fresh ginger root and add it to the tea for added anti-inflammatory properties.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional 5 minutes before straining out the solids.
Squeeze the juice of half a lime into the tea for a refreshing taste and an extra boost of vitamin C.
Drink the tea while still warm, up to three times a day, for natural relief of hiatus hernia symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried littleleaf linden flowers and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.

Astringent: The astringent properties of yarrow tea, attributed to tannins, can help tighten and tone the esophageal tissues, potentially reducing acid reflux associated with hiatus hernia.

remedy_recipe: Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow herb, adjusting the amount based on desired tea strength.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 1-2 minutes to ensure it's not too hot, which can damage the herb's beneficial compounds.
Place the measured yarrow herb in a tea infuser or teapot and pour the hot water over it.
